key_structures: TokenRefreshResponse struct for Google OAuth endpoint
file_insights.test_coverage: unit tests for credential JSON parsing and JS string extraction

file_insights.change: registered gemini provider in module tree

patterns_discovered.pattern: OAuth2 token refresh with expiry detection
patterns_discovered.details: handles expiry_date in both seconds and milliseconds (threshold check at year 3000), refreshes via Google token endpoint using refresh_token + client credentials

patterns_discovered.pattern: JS source string extraction without regex
patterns_discovered.details: extract_js_string_value() manually parses JavaScript variable assignments, handling both single and double quotes, whitespace normalization
`which gemini` → canonicalize() for symlink resolution → hierarchical npm package search (10 parent directories, both node_modules and lib/node_modules)
is_token_expired() returns false when expiry_date is None (assumes token valid), preventing premature refresh attempts
early returns with partial ProviderSnapshot on any error step (credentials read, OAuth extraction, token refresh), allows UI to show degraded state

gotchas_discovered.context: Google OAuth can return expiry in seconds or milliseconds; threshold check at 32503680000 (year 3000 in seconds) disambiguates
gotchas_discovered.risk: miscalculation would cause premature or missed token refreshes

gotchas_discovered.gotcha: OAuth client extraction depends on npm package structure
gotchas_discovered.context: oauth2.js path and variable names are fragile; changes to Gemini CLI npm package break extraction
gotchas_discovered.risk: medium - npm package structure can change, breaking silent detection
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: none yet - consider hardcoding or API fallback in subtask-2-2

gotchas_discovered.context: each Provider::fetch() call will re-request token refresh if expired, hitting Google API
gotchas_discovered.risk: rate limiting, latency, API quota waste
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: recommend caching refreshed token back to ~/.gemini/oauth_creds.json

gotchas_discovered.gotcha: Global npm installs require lib/node_modules search
gotchas_discovered.context: macOS global npm uses /usr/local/lib/node_modules; local installs use node_modules
gotchas_discovered.risk: asymmetric path search only mitigates macOS global installs

gotchas_discovered.gotcha: Quota fetch stub returns error, not empty state
gotchas_discovered.context: ProviderSnapshot error field populated with 'quota fetch not yet implemented'
gotchas_discovered.risk: UI will show error state even when detection succeeds
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: subtask-2-2 must implement actual quota API call

completed: GeminiCredentials struct with JSON serde mapping
detect() logic: checks oauth_creds.json file OR gemini binary on PATH
extract_oauth_client() walks up 10 parent dirs for npm package oauth2.js
refresh_token_if_expired() async POST to Google token endpoint
unit tests for all parsing logic (credentials, JS extraction)

recommendations.recommendation: Consider requesting Gemini CLI team for stable API to retrieve client credentials instead of scraping npm package; if scraping must continue, add test fixture with actual oauth2.js snapshot from multiple CLI versions
After refresh_token_if_expired() succeeds, write updated access_token and expiry_date back to ~/.gemini/oauth_creds.json to avoid repeated Google API calls within Provider::fetch() polling window
Document the 32503680000 threshold choice; consider extracting to constant MILLIS_THRESHOLD_SECONDS with comment explaining year 3000 derivation
Add integration test for actual Google token refresh (or mock endpoint); current unit tests only validate parsing logic, not round-trip OAuth flow

Provider::fetch() gracefully degrades on every error; consider retry logic for transient failures (e.g., token refresh 5xx), distinguish permanent failures (bad credentials) from temporary network issues
